title: A bizarre 'decapitated' asteroid likely made the moon's largest impact crater. NASA's Artemis astronauts may land near the proof
summary: A study suggests a decapitated asteroid likely created the moon's largest impact crater. NASA's Artemis astronauts may land near evidence of this ancient event.

title: A 29-pound Mars rock held NASA's Curiosity rover hostage for 6 days
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover was temporarily unable to move for six days due to a small Mars rock nicknamed 'Atacama' lodged in its wheel. The incident highlights the challenges of operating rovers on the Martian surface.

title: 'Saros' nails the cosmic horror and bullet-hell action assignments, but it falls short as a roguelite
summary: Saros delivers intense cosmic horror and bullet-hell gameplay but struggles as a roguelite. Housemarque's follow-up to Returnal showcases PS5's capabilities while feeling inconsistent.

title: Where does Earth's mysterious 'ring current' come from? NASA and the US Space Force are launching a mission to find out
summary: NASA and the US Space Force are collaborating on a mission to investigate Earth's mysterious 'ring current'. The space weather mission aims to study the phenomenon surrounding our planet.

title: 'Star Wars: Galactic Racer': Release date, trailers, gameplay & everything we know about the return of podracing
summary: The article discusses the upcoming release of 'Star Wars: Galactic Racer', covering details about its release date, trailers, and gameplay. It highlights the return of podracing, speeder racing, and speeder bike racing in the game.

title: Moon joy at Jupiter | Space photo of the day for May 7, 2026
summary: NASA's Juno spacecraft captured a photo of Jupiter's moon Thebe. This image highlights ongoing scientific exploration of Jupiter's moons.

title: Here's what 6 years of driving on Mars did to the wheels on NASA's Curiosity rover (video)
summary: A new video from NASA/JPL documents the wear and tear on the wheels of the Curiosity rover after six years of driving on Mars. The footage highlights the durability and challenges faced by the rover's mobility system on the Martian surface.

title: How do the biggest black holes in the universe form? Ripples in spacetime provide a clue
summary: Ripples in spacetime suggest that merging black holes and neutron stars have unusual oval orbits before colliding, which may challenge existing laws of physics. This discovery provides new insights into how the largest black holes in the universe form.

title: Artemis 2 moon launch brought nearly 350,000 people to Florida's Space Coast
summary: The Artemis 2 moon launch attracted approximately 346,000 visitors to Florida's Space Coast, comparable to the population of Honolulu, Hawaii. This event highlights the significant public interest in NASA's lunar mission.

title: Should Saturn's huge moon Titan be humanity's next destination, after the moon and Mars?
summary: A first-of-its-kind gathering next month will plan a crewed mission to Titan, Saturn's largest moon. This follows discussions about Titan as a potential destination after the Moon and Mars.

title: Meet the Fleet: NASA Armstrong Continues Legacy of Flight Research
summary: NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center has expanded its fleet with new aircraft, including two F-15 supersonic jets and a Pilatus PC-12 utility plane, to support science and aeronautics missions. The center continues the legacy of flight research pioneers like Neil Armstrong.

title: NASA Welcomes Paraguay as 67th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Paraguay became the 67th signatory to the Artemis Accords during a ceremony in Asunción, committing to principles for civil space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed the nation's participation in the international agreement.

title: NASA Sends Mars Helicopter Blades Beyond Mach 1
summary: NASA tested next-generation Mars helicopter rotor blades at high speeds, finding they could surpass Mach 1 without breaking. The tests were conducted at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory in November 2025.

title: NASA’s Next-Gen Mars Helicopter Rotors Are Moving Fast
summary: NASA is testing next-generation Mars helicopter rotors at supersonic speeds in the 25-Foot Space Simulator at JPL. The tests involve advanced rotor blades designed to improve performance on future Mars missions.

title: NASA Pushes Next-Gen Mars Helicopter Rotor Blades Past Mach 1
summary: NASA's next-generation Mars helicopter rotor blades achieved supersonic speeds during tests at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory. The blades were tested in a chamber simulating Martian conditions.

title: Industry Moon Lander Training Cabin Lands at NASA for Artemis
summary: A full-scale mock-up of Blue Origin's Blue Moon Mark 2 crew cabin for NASA's Artemis program has been delivered for training and testing. The cabin will be used for mission simulations as NASA prepares for future lunar landings.

title: A Light in the Dark
summary: A photo taken during the Artemis II mission on April 3, 2026, shows Earth's edge illuminated against space. Artemis II was NASA's first crewed flight to test deep space capabilities ahead of future lunar missions.

title: NASA-Supported Small Spacecraft Launches to Study Solar Particles
summary: A university-designed small spacecraft named SNAPPY, supported by NASA, launched aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket to study solar neutrinos. The mission aims to analyze particles moving at near-light speeds to advance astro-particle physics.

title: NASA’s Prithvi Becomes First AI Geospatial Foundation Model In Orbit
summary: NASA and IBM's open-source Prithvi Geospatial AI foundation model has been demonstrated in orbit, marking the first such deployment. The model was tested aboard two in-orbit platforms.

title: NASA’s Simulated Mars Mission Marks 200 Days Inside Habitat
summary: NASA's simulated Mars mission crew has completed 200 days of their 378-day mission inside a habitat. The crew is currently experiencing a two-week communications blackout to simulate Mars-Earth signal loss.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing its commercial sector towards reusability in space missions.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion work as Flight 12 approaches, six months after the conclusion of Flight 11. The old design of Pad 1 is being updated for future missions.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission, potentially marking a calm period before a busier launch schedule. The launches are highlighted as part of a space industry update.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched its third rideshare mission of 2026, deploying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit aboard a Falcon 9 rocket.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight with a revised trajectory. The return of Starship launches is expected within weeks.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully launched its new Soyuz-5 rocket on its maiden flight. The launch marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor, with experience in mass casualty events and overseas service, discusses their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The mission highlights the intersection of medical expertise and space exploration.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant milestone for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the recent Artemis II launch, which marked a historic milestone with four astronauts flying by the Moon.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy, ULA's Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's LEO satellites this week. Six launches are planned from Florida, California, and French Guiana.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but the specific details of the news were not provided in the input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication commemorates five decades of space exploration milestones.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a test involving a parachute bake-out, likely related to a space mission or technology development. This process involves heating the parachute to remove moisture and ensure proper functionality.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life beneath the Martian surface while adhering to planetary protection protocols.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to share feedback at the SME Forum 2026, part of SME and Midcap Days held on 8–9 June 2026 at ESA Headquarters in Paris. The event includes an online preparatory forum in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement is highlighted as ideal for navigation purposes.

title: South Korea Pushes to Commercialize Quantum Research
summary: South Korea is accelerating efforts to commercialize quantum research, aiming to establish itself as a key player in the emerging quantum technology sector. The country is positioning itself to compete in the global quantum industry.

title: Overview Energy Wins Air Force Contract
summary: Overview Energy secured an Air Force contract to provide continuous power to military bases using space-based solar energy, addressing vulnerabilities in fuel supply chains.

title: Astranis Raises $450M+, Including $300M Series E
summary: Astranis has secured over $450 million in funding, including a $300 million Series E round led by Snowpoint Ventures and Franklin Templeton, valuing the company at $2.8 billion.

title: Scout Space Closes $18M Series A
summary: Scout Space has secured $18 million in Series A funding to expand its operations, including building a new satellite sensor factory and nearly doubling its workforce in the next 18 months.

title: LiveEO Raises €28M+ to Fund its First Constellation
summary: LiveEO, a German company, has raised over €28 million to fund its first satellite constellation. The funds will be used to scale operations, launch the constellation, and develop technology to meet increasing demand for Earth observation (EO) data.

title: Commerce Aiming to Open Novel Space Applications This Summer
summary: The Office of Space Commerce (OSC) plans to streamline licensing for innovative space missions by this summer. This initiative aims to position OSC as a central hub for novel space applications.

title: Bridenstine Takes Over as Quantum Space CEO
summary: Jim Bridenstine, former NASA administrator, has been appointed CEO of Quantum Space. He expressed enthusiasm for returning to national security and defense work, aligning with his background.

title: Anduril Names Its SBI Team For Golden Dome
summary: Anduril has named its team for the Space-Based Interceptors (SBI) component of the Golden Dome missile defense architecture. The company is one of 12 prime contractors involved in this initiative.

title: ESA Taps Edge Aerospace for Space Cloud Contract
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has selected Edge Aerospace, a Luxembourg-based company, to develop an architecture and roadmap for orbital data centers under a new space cloud contract.

title: Lt. Gen. Schiess Nominated to Lead Space Force
summary: Lt. Gen. Schiess has been nominated to lead the U.S. Space Force. If confirmed, he would become the third chief of space operations since the Space Force's establishment in 2019.

title: Skyroot Secures $60M in Funding, Becoming India’s First Space ‘Unicorn’
summary: Skyroot Aerospace secured $60 million in funding, becoming India's first space 'unicorn'. The Indian launch startup is a rising star in the country's space economy.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M, Pricing at High End for IPO
summary: HawkEye 360 raised $416 million in its IPO, becoming the second space company to go public this year. The signals intelligence company began trading on the stock market.

title: Spire Says New German Facility Can Produce Up to 100 Satellites Per Year
summary: Spire has opened a new satellite manufacturing facility in Munich with the capacity to produce up to 100 satellites per year. The facility is designed for end-to-end satellite production.

title: MDA Space Satellite Revenue Grows 40% in Q1, Greenley Says ‘No Change’ Due to Globalstar Deal
summary: MDA Space reported a 40% year-over-year revenue growth in its satellite manufacturing business for Q1 2026, driven by projects like Telesat Lightspeed. CEO Mike Greenley stated that the Globalstar deal had no impact on these financial results.

title: Enabled by AI, NGA Director Focused on Mission, Transformation, and Workforce
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) is leveraging AI to enhance its mission, transformation, and workforce in response to evolving threats and warfighter needs. The agency's new director is prioritizing resilience and the adoption of emerging technologies.

title: Telespazio to Distribute Greenerwave Multi-Orbit Terminals to European Defense Market
summary: Telespazio France will distribute multi-orbit terminals developed by Greenerwave to the European defense market under a new agreement. The deal aims to enhance connectivity solutions for defense applications.

title: Verizon Details ‘Satellite-Everywhere’ for Disaster Response, Expands Satellite Fleet
summary: Verizon is expanding its satellite fleet to enhance disaster response capabilities and aims for a 'satellite-everywhere' failover strategy. The move is part of the company's broader efforts to improve connectivity during emergencies.

title: Astranis Raises $300M in Series E as Part of $450M in New Capital
summary: Astranis, a satellite manufacturer, raised $300 million in a Series E funding round and secured an additional $155 million in a credit facility to boost satellite production. The total new capital amounts to $450 million.

title: SWISSto12 Strikes German Partnership Deal for D2D GEO Satellite
summary: SWISSto12 has formed a new partnership deal in Germany to enhance media broadcast capabilities for Geostationary (GEO) satellites. The collaboration focuses on high-performance space subsystems for Direct-to-Device (D2D) satellite services.

title: Anduril to Work With Impulse Space, K2 Space, Voyager, and Others on Space-Based Interceptor Award
summary: Anduril Industries has formed a team of collaborators including Impulse Space, K2 Space, and others to prototype space-based interceptor technologies. The collaboration follows Anduril's recent award to develop these technologies.
